title: lonchocarpus benthamianus
intro: Lonchocarpus benthamianus, also known as the "Laguna tree" or "Caoba", is a plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae, native to Central America and southern Mexico. The medicinal properties of Lonchocarpus benthamianus are attributed to its seeds, which contain a toxic alkaloid called rotenone, known for its insecticidal and anti-inflammatory properties. The seeds have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to their health benefits of reducing inflammation, fighting bacteria, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. Lonchocarpus benthamianus is often used in herbal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties, although it is essential to use it under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its toxicity.
